name: lisa-globalfit-framework
documentation:
# LISA GlobalFit Framework

## Purpose

This is a prototype implementation of the LISA GlobalFit Framework.

* [Development guide](docs/dev.md)
* [Architecture](docs/architecture.md)

classes.name: lisa.globalfit.framework.waveform.Waveform

methods.name: lisa.globalfit.framework.waveform.Waveform.evaluate
documentation:
Evaluate the waveform at given points.

```
param x

Array of points at which to evaluate the waveform.

return

Array containing the values of the waveform at the given points.
```
Abstract base class representing a waveform.

modules.name: lisa.globalfit.framework.modules
documentation: Import all modules so that they can register themselvses to the registry.

classes.name: lisa.globalfit.framework.modules.module.ModuleBase

methods.name: lisa.globalfit.framework.modules.module.ModuleBase.__init__
documentation:
Create base module.

```
param datafile

Input data file containing signal.

param output_checkpoint

Path to a save state, from which execution can be
resumed after nominal termination or a crash.

param channel_name

Topic prefix to use when talking on the bus.

param bus

Event bus interface.
```
Abstract Global Fit module.

classes.name: lisa.globalfit.framework.modules.stub.module.StubModule
documentation: Placeholder module.

classes.name: lisa.globalfit.framework.model.ParametricModel

methods.name: lisa.globalfit.framework.model.ParametricModel.__eq__
documentation:
Check whether two models are equivalent.

Use with caution!

This is more of a helper for unit tests than a real feature, since it performs
equality checks on floating point arrays.

Well-known parametric model.

```
param name

Identifier of the model.

param parameters

Parameters of the model.
```

classes.name: lisa.globalfit.framework.model.MarkovChainSamples
documentation:
Sequence of Markov chain samples.

```
param idx_iteration

Current Global Fit iteration.

param

idx_step_end: Step of the last element in waveforms parameters.

param waveforms

Model of the waveform. Its name represents the function identifier
of the associated waveform generator. Its parameters represent the samples and
have a shape of n_steps * n_walkers * n_dim. Its size is estimated to be of the
following order of magnitude.

1000 (steps) * 20 (parameters) * 5 (walkers) * 4 (f32) ~ 400kB.
```

classes.name: lisa.globalfit.framework.msg.data.DataMessage
documentation: Message for passing metadata between modules.

classes.name: lisa.globalfit.framework.msg.data.ModuleState
documentation:
Comprehensive state of a module.

This state can be used as module checkpoint, from which MCMC sampling can be
resumed.

```
param chain

Samples leading to current state.
```

classes.name: lisa.globalfit.framework.msg.data.ErrorMessage
documentation: Message for reporting errors.

classes.name: lisa.globalfit.framework.msg.base.MessageBase

methods.name: lisa.globalfit.framework.msg.base.MessageBase.encode
documentation:
Encode the message for communication on the message bus.

Implementations should return a binary representation of the message, using
Python pickle serialization protocol.

```
return

Encoded message.
```

methods.name: lisa.globalfit.framework.msg.base.MessageBase.decode
documentation:
Decode a message from its binary representation.

```
param data

Binary content of the encoded message.

return

Decoded message.
```
Base class for event messages exchanged within the framework.

classes.name: lisa.globalfit.framework.msg.control.ControlMessage
documentation: Message for controlling Global Fit flow.

classes.name: lisa.globalfit.framework.msg.control.ConfigureModule
documentation: Configure an initialized module.

classes.name: lisa.globalfit.framework.msg.control.IterateModule
documentation:
Iterate a module for a given number of steps.

```
param idx_iteration

Current iteration counter.

param detections

Live catalog.

param step_count

Number of steps to execute in iteration.
```

classes.name: lisa.globalfit.framework.msg.control.ContinueModule
documentation: Finish the current step and wait for next iteration order.

classes.name: lisa.globalfit.framework.msg.control.TerminateModule
documentation: Finish the current step and exit.

classes.name: lisa.globalfit.framework.msg.control.KillModule
documentation: Exit immediately.

classes.name: lisa.globalfit.framework.msg.control.ExecutionStateUpdate
documentation: Transition of execution state.

classes.name: lisa.globalfit.framework.msg.control.ModuleGroupConfiguration
documentation: Configuration of a module group.

modules.name: lisa.globalfit.framework.logging
documentation: Define the logging levels that the user can choose

classes.name: lisa.globalfit.framework.catalog.SourceCatalog
documentation: Gravitational wave detection catalog.

functions.name: lisa.globalfit.framework.signal.read_dataset_array
documentation:
Read a dataset from an HDF5 file to an in-memory array.

```
param file

File to read from.

param path

Path to dataset in file.

raises ValueError

If the path does not exist in the file or is not a dataset.

return

Array containing dataset values.
```

classes.name: lisa.globalfit.framework.signal.NoisyGaussianMixture

methods.name: lisa.globalfit.framework.signal.NoisyGaussianMixture.n_distributions
documentation:
Count the number of distributions in the signal.

```
return

Number of distributions.
```

methods.name: lisa.globalfit.framework.signal.NoisyGaussianMixture.write_dataset
documentation:
Write the signal to a HDF5 file.

```
param output

File to write to.
```

methods.name: lisa.globalfit.framework.signal.NoisyGaussianMixture.plot
documentation:
Plot the signal to an image file.

```
param file

File to write image to.
```

methods.name: lisa.globalfit.framework.signal.NoisyGaussianMixture.read_dataset
documentation:
Read a dataset from a HDF5 file.

```
param file

File to read from.
```

Sum of gaussians with noise.

```
param means

Array of shape (n_distributions,) with true mean values.

param variances

Array of shape (n_distributions,) with true variance values.

param noise

Array of shape (n_samples,) with noise values. Implicitly uses the
same time points as the signal.

param signal

Array of shape (2, n_samples) with time points and noisy signal
values.
```
